data.title: Fda aware of Erbe USA asking removal of certain flexible cryoprobes from customers
TSX rises on prospects of Mideast truce
data.description:
March 25 (Reuters) - Canada's main stock index advanced on Wednesday as signs of potential de-escalation in the Middle East conflict helped allay investor concerns about extended disruptions to energy supplies.

At 10:49 a.m. ET, the Toronto Stock Exchange's S&P/TSX composite index was up 1.3% at 32,358.13.At 10:49 a.m. ET, the Toronto Stock Exchange's S&P/TSX composite index was up 1.3% at 32,358.13.

Pakistan has delivered a U.S. proposal to defuse tensions and help end the war in the Gulf, with either Islamabad or Turkey playing potential hosts if the talks went ahead, an Iranian official told Reuters on Wednesday. However, Tehran has publicly denied it will negotiate with the Trump administration.

"The markets are anticipating... that a full-out de-escalation and ceasefire... will actually happen and then we can hopefully start to stabilize what this three-week struggle has been," said Shiraz Ahmed, founder at Sartorial Wealth. "If we're able to get a ceasefire and a meaningful one soon, then it may help limit sort of the long term damage."

Signs of diplomatic progress lifted sentiment, with nine of eleven sectors on the TSX trading in green. The materials sector led gains, up 2.5%, as gold prices firmed on a weaker dollar. [GOL/]

Oil prices, meanwhile, retreated on hopes that a potential ceasefire may help normalize flows through the Strait of Hormuz, a key gateway for about a fifth of the world's energy supplies. [O/R]. Energy shares were down 0.3%, tracking crude.

A pullback in energy prices could ease pressure on central banks, even as geopolitical risks threaten to stoke inflationary fears. Traders expect the Bank of Canada to hold rates steady in April and have priced in two hikes by year-end, LSEG data showed.

The S&P/TSX information technology index rose 1.5%, in line with gains in the tech-heavy Nasdaq on Wall Street.

Dollarama rose 1.7% after CIBC upgraded the retailer to "outperformer" from "neutral," lifting the consumer staples sector.

(Reporting by Rashika Singh in Bengaluru; Editing by Diti Pujara)

By Rashika Singh

Novo Nordisk seeks to block Dr Reddy's launch of semaglutide generic in India
data.description:
Danish pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk has filed a lawsuit in a Delhi court to prevent Dr Reddy's from launching a generic version of semaglutide under the brand name Olymviq, which it claims is too similar to Ozempic.

The news was reported by the Indian publication Mint.

The legal action comes as patents are set to expire in India, paving the way for lower-cost generic alternatives. The court has ordered Dr Reddy's to suspend manufacturing and sales pending a hearing scheduled for Friday.

China Taiping Insurance Holdings Company Limited Reports Earnings Results for the Full Year Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
China Taiping Insurance Holdings Company Limited reported earnings results for the full year ended December 31, 2025. For the full year, the company reported net income was HKD 27,059.28 million compared to HKD 8,431.61 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was HKD 7.251 compared to HKD 2.068 a year ago.

Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was HKD 7.251 compared to HKD 2.068 a year ago.

Lockheed Martin Enters Agreement with Department of War to Accelerate Precision Strike Missile Production
data.description:
Lockheed Martin and the Department of War announced a framework agreement to accelerate the production of Precision Strike Missiles (PrSM). The agreement builds on a previous $4.94 billion contract award from the U.S. Army last year and together, these actions will quadruple PrSM production capacity. This initiative directly supports the national imperative to build a more lethal, resilient and ready fighting force, backed by a stronger, advanced industrial base.

The agreement includes the ability to negotiate a multi-year contract up to seven years, should Congress grant multi-year authority, contingent upon future congressional authorization. The agreement marks the latest commitment Lockheed Martin has made to expand and strengthen the Arsenal of Freedom, creating high-paying, highly skilled jobs that will secure peace through strength for generations to come.

data.title: Guming Holdings Limited Reports Earnings Results for the Full Year Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
Guming Holdings Limited reported earnings results for the full year ended December 31, 2025. For the full year, the company reported sales was CNY 12,913.77 million compared to CNY 8,791.36 million a year ago. Net income was CNY 3,109.12 million compared to CNY 1,478.51 million a year ago.

Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 1.34 compared to CNY 0.71 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 1.08 compared to CNY 0.71 a year ago.

Cintas Corporation Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended February 28, 2026
data.description:
Cintas Corporation reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended February 28, 2026. For the third quarter, the company reported sales was USD 2,177.45 million compared to USD 2,021.14 million a year ago. Revenue was USD 2,841.44 million compared to USD 2,609.16 million a year ago. Net income was USD 502.5 million compared to USD 463.5 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 1.25 compared to USD 1.14 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 1.24 compared to USD 1.13 a year ago.
For the nine months, sales was USD 6,423.92 million compared to USD 5,945.39 million a year ago. Revenue was USD 8,359.56 million compared to USD 7,672.53 million a year ago. Net income was USD 1,488.98 million compared to USD 1,364.03 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 3.7 compared to USD 3.37 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 3.65 compared to USD 3.31 a year ago.

data.title: Paychex, Inc. Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended February 28, 2026
data.description:
Paychex, Inc. reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended February 28, 2026. For the third quarter, the company reported revenue was USD 1,808.9 million compared to USD 1,509 million a year ago. Net income was USD 560.3 million compared to USD 519.3 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 1.56 compared to USD 1.44 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 1.56 compared to USD 1.43 a year ago.
For the nine months, revenue was USD 4,906.5 million compared to USD 4,144.4 million a year ago. Net income was USD 1,339.5 million compared to USD 1,360.1 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 3.73 compared to USD 3.78 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 3.71 compared to USD 3.76 a year ago.

India retains headline inflation target at 4% following review
data.description:
NEW DELHI, March 25 (Reuters) - India's federal government on Wednesday retained the target for retail inflation at 4%, within a comfort band of 2%-6%, according to an official notification.

India adopted the inflation targeting framework in 2016, formally tasking the RBI with keeping headline consumer price inflation within a band set by the government. The framework was last reviewed in 2021.

The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee, which includes three members from the central bank and three government appointees, are tasked with meeting that target.

(Reporting by Nikunj Ohri and Sakshi Dayal; Editing by Alison Williams)
